text.tspan: The choice is per-operation in Cassandra and DynamoDB:
you can write with ONE for fast logging and read with QUORUM for correctness,
or vice versa, on the same data u2014 trading consistency for latency per query.
+ tolerates 2 node failures for both reads and writes
+ writes wait for 3 acks (medium latency)
+ reads query 3 nodes (medium latency)
+ Cassandra QUORUM, MongoDB majority
+ reads only need 2 acks u2192 fast
+ many nodes can serve reads
u2212 writes need 4 u2192 slower & need 4 healthy nodes
u2212 only tolerates 1 write failure
+ writes need only 2 acks u2192 fast
+ tolerates 3 write failures
u2212 reads need 4 nodes u2192 slow + sensitive to failures
u2212 used in Dynamo, voldemort for write-heavy workloads
u2192 NO QUORUM GUARANTEE u2014 reads can see stale data
u2192 Cassandra ONE; only OK if app tolerates staleness
u2192 fastest, most available, weakest
+ writes go to all nodes u2192 any single read gets latest
u2212 a single failed node blocks all writes
u2192 unrealistic for fault tolerance
